subject: Ecology
title: Urban Food Webs
description: A quick win: understand Urban Food Webs.

hook.text: Cities have food webs too—just with dumpsters and bird feeders mixed into the chain.

buildup.text: Rats eat garbage. Hawks eat rats. Peregrine falcons hunt pigeons from skyscraper ledges.

discovery.text: Human food waste subsidizes urban wildlife, boosting populations far above natural levels.

twist.text: Fed wildlife loses wariness. Coyotes that eat trash become bolder around people.

climax.text: Managing urban food webs means managing human waste as much as wildlife.

punchline.text: Our leftovers rewrite the food chain.

quiz.question: How does human food waste affect urban wildlife?
options: It boosts populations above natural levels
